action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 17 eval scored rmse_z500=1032.17 with the retrained model, which is worse than the best-ever 1018.63 from iter 5. The retrained model achieved lower val_loss (0.110 vs 0.117) so it should be better, but predict.py now applies test-time augmentation (TTA) with 4 longitudinal rolls that wasn't present in iter 5. Since the model wasn't trained with longitudinal augmentation, TTA likely hurts performance by averaging over out-of-distribution shifted inputs. We have ~25 minutes remaining. Analysis: Before burning time on retraining, we should verify locally whether TTA is causing the regression. If removing TTA drops local val RMSE close to or below 1018, we can simply update predict.py to disable TTA and resubmit the same checkpoint. Decision: Run a quick local evaluation on val_data with and without TTA to measure the exact impact.
